title: Enable more granular sorting for Gantt View Widget
excerpt:

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
As suggested by Dani I am creating a new topic to request a feature.

As described here in the FAQ topic, I would like:

  1. an understanding how a Gantt View Widget which is grouped by person is sorted

  2. avoid automatic resorting

  3. influence the order in which people are listed

On this note it would also be great to have more granular influence over the order in widgets in general. For example are subitems not automatically listed below their parent item nor are they grouped together. Instead more often than not one or several subitems of different parent items are interspersed between.

Thank you!

posts.author: ccrouse (Charles)
posts.cookedHtml: Did this topic ever get solved? I’m struggling with the same thing

posts.author: Jeppe_aety (Jeppe van der Kamp)
posts.cookedHtml: Is this a feature getting worked on? I’ve had several clients asking about the option to sort the grouping in both Gantt and Timeline Widgets

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: I have not received any news on this. It should at least be possible to let us know how to order of people is being decided.

posts.author: najoconsulting (Neal Altman)
posts.cookedHtml: No response from @Monday.com but seems like an easy fix. Will make the to do list they advertise much more usable!

posts.author: IsaIsa (Isa)
posts.cookedHtml:
This would be really useful for my team as well! We currently group our items by Priority, with P0-P3 as status options. It would be great to sort the group order in the Gantt view so that the priorities are ordered in the same hierarchy as my statuses.

Right now the group order doesn’t ascend from P0-P3 and it looks quite unprofessional to important stakeholders who rely on this view:

Screenshot 2024-07-30 at 4.13.23 PM1138×620 26 KB

posts.author: GanttGo (Hidai Bar-Mor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone,

we just launched GanttGo, a marketplace app that addresses several pain points mentioned in this thread:

  • Sub-items stay grouped under their parent- In GanttGo, sub-items are always displayed directly below their parent task keeping your hierarchi intact and easy to follow.
  • Manual group ordering- You can move groups up/down to arrange them exactly how you want. This gives you direct control over how people or categories are listed in your Gantt view.
  • Order preservation- We respect your monday’s board native ordering, so tasks don’t get automatically reshuffled when you open the chart. Also you can add items from GanttGo and decide to which group to add them to.

We have on our roadmap:

  • sort tasks by date
  • sort alphabetically by task name
  • sort by status or duration
  • Ascending/descending toggle

We’d love to hear which of those is the most valuable to you- it will help us prioritize.

GanttGo is available on monday’s marketplace if you want to try it out.
